page: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive, GPU-accelerated server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8559C x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at 2.4 GHz, supported by a 320 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz.

For local storage, the instance is equipped with a 1900 GB NVMe SSD. Network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. The Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8), which accelerate cryptographic, vector, and matrix operations directly on the CPU.

In benchmark evaluations, the g7e.4xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in single-threaded tasks, including gzip compression at 35.02 GB/sec and a Geekbench single-core score of 2336.0. Its GPU and memory architecture excel in large language model (LLM) inference, achieving top-tier speeds of 1318.31 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 31.01 tokens/sec for text generation on a large 70B model. Multi-core CPU operations, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, machine learning workloads, and database applications requiring high-speed local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated, graphics-intensive server built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8559C x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD. Acceleration is driven by a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM. The system delivers 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and supports advanced CPU instructions like AMX and AVX-512. In benchmarks, the g7e.4xlarge achieves top-tier results in single-threaded operations and LLM inference, processing 1318.31 tokens/sec for a 70B model prompt. Average-tier performance is observed in multi-core processing and database operations. This hardware combination is optimized for large language model inference, graphics rendering, and data-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an Intel Xeon CPU, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with an Intel Xeon CPU, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server featuring Blackwell architecture, high-capacity VRAM, and local NVMe storage for advanced AI inference and graphics workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 320 MB L3 cache
NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM
128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 system memory
1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier LLM inference performance across small, medium, and large models
